goal: Implement PRD v2 Phase 1 for VocabSpark by delivering the Today task page, vocabulary filtering, review scheduling, and enforceable daily quota behavior.
scope: Today task page UI and related state flow
Vocabulary filter logic and filter controls
Review scheduler logic and persistence wiring
Daily quota calculation, enforcement, and reset behavior
Relevant API/service layer and tests for Phase 1 features
constraints: Limit changes to Phase 1 surfaces only; do not expand into Phase 2+ features.
Preserve existing data models and public APIs unless a minimal backward-compatible extension is required.
Avoid introducing schema-breaking migrations without explicit fallback handling.
Keep scheduler and quota logic deterministic and timezone-aware for daily reset semantics.
Do not degrade existing learning/review flows unrelated to Today page and filters.
Ensure filtering and quota enforcement are applied consistently across UI and backend/service boundaries.
acceptance_tests: Today page renders today's workload correctly and updates after completing or skipping an item.
Vocab filters (e.g., status/tag/difficulty if defined in PRD) return the expected subset and persist expected UI state during session.
Review scheduler selects due items according to PRD rules and does not include non-due items.
Daily quota caps new/review actions at configured limit and blocks excess actions with correct UX messaging.
Daily quota resets at day boundary in configured timezone and allows new actions after reset.
Regression checks confirm pre-existing study flows still work when no filters are active and quota is not reached.
implementation_steps: Audit PRD v2 Phase 1 requirements and map each requirement to existing modules, identifying gaps in Today page, filters, scheduler, and quota logic.
Implement/adjust domain logic for review scheduling and daily quota enforcement in service layer with clear pure-function boundaries where possible.
Wire Today page and vocab filter UI to new/updated selectors and actions, ensuring consistent empty/loading/error states.
Integrate backend/service responses so quota and scheduling outcomes are reflected immediately in UI interactions.
Add/extend automated tests for scheduler correctness, quota boundary/reset behavior, filter correctness, and Today-page end-to-end user flows.
Run full regression and polish edge cases (timezone rollover, no due items, quota reached mid-session), then finalize implementation notes.
summary: Phase 1 will be delivered by aligning UI and service logic around deterministic review scheduling, strict daily quota enforcement, and a reliable Today workflow, validated by focused functional and regression tests.
